economic_notes: Nation receives 42% of revenues from ISC (down from 47% prior year). Development Corporation (Buffalo Point Development Corp. Ltd. and 5426831 Manitoba Ltd.) consolidated. Land lease revenue significant at $2,008,382. Marina, golf, accommodations, restaurant operations on reserve. VLT gaming revenue present. Property tax regime in place.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2022 (not 2024). Province is Manitoba, not Alberta. Four entities consolidated: Buffalo Point First Nation Housing Authority, Buffalo Point Development Corp. Ltd., Buffalo Point First Nation, and 5426831 Manitoba Ltd. Deferred revenue of $11.1M is largely lot lease prepayments recognized over lease terms of 1-69 years, making revenue figures complex. Promissory note of $198,623 payable to Receiver General is interest-free and overdue as of March 31, 2022 with renewal pending. Contaminated sites exist with no liability recorded. Capital lease obligations of $175,420 excluded from long_term_debt figure. No schedule of remuneration included in extracted text. ISC funding calculated as ISC ($2,076,923) + ISC Health ($1,951,382) = $4,028,305. Total government transfers includes ISC + ISC Health + CMHC + TLEC + First Peoples Development Inc. Cash paid to employees ($2,893,609) used as proxy for salaries total.
